title: cassia viarum
intro: Cassia viarum, also known as the Wild Cassia or Parijat, is a tropical flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cassia viarum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Cassia viarum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to make various tonics and remedies.
